rows.claim: The frozen throughput.batch=4 is an inherited bench-harness default, not a measured choice; it scheduling-starves the GPU and costs >=1 wall-day on the v0 pretrain.
rows.equation_or_invariant: wall_days = T / (B*S / (t_c(B) + p)) / 86400; t_c sublinear in B until compute saturation; raw tok/s plateau marks the eager compute ceiling
rows.baseline_provenance: receipts/fp19-bench-20260611T024648Z.json results.c03-qat; same-day reproduced anchor 17899.0 (-4.48% drift, within 10% tolerance) in receipts/fp32-step-econ-20260611T142831Z.json
rows.intervention: raise batch 4 -> 16 (conservative; 10.83 GiB free) or 24 (receipted ceiling; 5.15 GiB free) with grad-ckpt ON, eager, governor unchanged; lands ONLY as a registered deviation to configs/v0-pretrain-config.json after E1b loss-match on real shards
rows.kill_condition: best safe cell < reproduced_anchor * 1.05, or E1b loss-at-token-budget worse at equal lr and at scaled lr

measured_after.confound_note: single variable changed vs anchor (batch); ckpt ON + eager in every compared cell; governor identical (0.80 fraction / 1.5 GiB margin / 0.05 s pace)

rows.claim: The 0.05 s/step pace floor is a 22% wall tax at batch 4 because it is amortized over only 4096 tokens; it is a duty-cycle rail, so the fix is amortization, never loosening.
rows.equation_or_invariant: tax = p / (t_c(B) + p), p = 0.05 s/step fixed floor
rows.metric: pacing_tax (fraction of generation/train wall spent in pace sleeps)
rows.baseline_provenance: receipts/fp19-bench-20260611T024648Z.json (1 - 18737.7/24294.7); reproduced 0.2185 in fp32-step-econ-20260611T142831Z.json b4 cell
rows.intervention: same as R1 (batch amortization; pace constant untouched)
rows.expected_sign: tax down toward p/(t_c(B)+p) at larger B
rows.kill_condition: inseparable from R1 by construction; killed iff R1 is killed

rows.claim: torch.compile, promised by the frozen config's throughput block, fails on this exact daemon environment (torch 2.6.0+cu124 x transformers output_capturing wrapper) -> the v0 trainer WILL take the config's receipted eager-fallback deviation; eager raw ~25-27k tok/s is the real planning ceiling.
rows.equation_or_invariant: foregone kernel-fusion speedup; eager raw plateau ~=26k tok/s at B>=8 marks the no-compile ceiling
rows.baseline: CELL-ERROR: NameError name 'torch' is not defined (dynamo tracing inside transformers/utils/output_capturing.py)
rows.baseline_provenance: receipts/fp32-step-econ-20260611T142831Z.json b24-ckpt-compile cell; full traceback in daemon job 93e74934 log
rows.intervention: none pre-launch (env surgery is unreceipted risk on the critical path); next-rung: pin a compile-safe transformers version or export the step to a plain nn.Module, prove with a compile-cell-OK fp32-step-econ receipt
rows.expected_sign: compile cell OK with tok_s_raw above eager plateau
rows.kill_condition: a compile-OK cell shows <=5% over eager (fusion not worth the env risk)

rows.claim: KILLED: 9p (/mnt/b) loader throughput starves the v0 trainer.
rows.equation_or_invariant: required_io = T * 2 bytes / wall_s ~= 50 KB/s sustained vs 9p sequential >= tens of MB/s: >=3 orders of magnitude headroom
rows.metric: required sustained read MB/s vs available
rows.baseline: ~0.05 MB/s required (6.97e9 tokens * 2 B over >=3.35 days)
rows.intervention: explicit no-change diagnosis (recorded so nobody optimizes it)

rows.claim: Synchronous checkpoint writes to /mnt/b stall the GPU ~30-35 s per checkpoint (~5 GB state over ~150 MB/s 9p); binds only if cadence < ~10 min.
rows.equation_or_invariant: stall_tax = ckpt_bytes / io_bw / cadence_s; >5% iff cadence < ~10 min at 5 GB
rows.metric: checkpoint wall seconds / segment wall seconds
rows.baseline: unmeasured (no real v0 checkpoint exists yet); size estimate ~5 GB from 0.368e9 params (bf16 weights + fp32 AdamW moments + Muon momentum)
rows.baseline_provenance: hypothesis (param-count arithmetic); measure at first P-own-resume receipt
rows.intervention: if it binds: write checkpoint to WSL-native ext4, async copy to B:; spec note routed to Eli with eng-54
rows.kill_condition: first P-own-resume receipt shows ckpt wall <=5% of segment wall

rows.claim: Round-loop wall is generation-dominated ~10:1 and the whole-batch HF generate holds 8 KV slots until the LAST stream stops; short mean completions + max_new=512 straggler => most decode slots idle.
rows.equation_or_invariant: slot_waste = 1 - mean(len_i) / max(len_i) per batch; round_wall ~= gen_wall (gen:train = 9.78:1)
rows.metric: verified episodes per generation-minute; gen:train wall ratio
rows.intervention: owned-core round-1 sampler built with bucketed lengths / per-stream stop / batch refill from day one (spec to Eli); NO patch to the borrowed-core loop (fallback-(a) track only)
rows.expected_sign: verified episodes per gen-minute up >=2x at equal governor pacing
rows.kill_condition: round-1 sampling wall < 10% of round wall at v0 scale (0.37B decodes ~10x faster than 3B) -> deprioritize

rows.claim: KILLED: round-loop adapter training is a bottleneck.
Round verdicts are STAT-limited, not GPU-limited: at the frozen round-gate N=100, effects below ~11 pp (disc 0.2) are invisible regardless of GPU-hours spent sampling.
rows.metric: CI95 width (pp) of arm-minus-base deltas; MDE at N=100
rows.baseline: measured widths 16.3-20.9 pp at N=43 (live w4 instrument); at N=100: half-width 8.8 pp, MDE80 11.1 pp at disc 0.2
rows.intervention: none pre-launch (N=100 frozen in fp-27 prereg; changing it now = goalpost-moving); round-2 prereg sizes N from the MDE table or adopts group-sequential
rows.expected_sign: MDE down at fixed compute via design, not via more GPU sampling
rows.kill_condition: round-1 verdict CI decisively one-sided despite predicted width (effects at this rung large enough that power never binds)

rows.claim: Episode DIVERSITY per GPU-hour, not raw verified-signal density, is the binding round-loop variable: fp-25 receipts show +75.9 pp in-dist at the SAME verified-bit budget that produced the transfer-ceiling null.
rows.equation_or_invariant: transfer gain = f(distribution coverage of verified episodes), not f(verified bits) at fixed coverage
rows.metric: in-dist gain vs floor-scoped transfer at fixed sampling budget; sampler_valued_bits/gen-min as the density instrument
rows.baseline: in-dist +75.9 pp vs transfer ceiling (null) at equal budget; density instrument exists: 32.7-40.3 bits/gen-min (A1)
rows.intervention: decided by fp-27 round-1 verdicts (L1+L2 grammar widens coverage by construction; fp27b executor merged): GAIN with L2-included sampling at fixed budget supports diversity; FLAT with rising density supports neither
rows.expected_sign: round-1 GAIN at fixed GPU budget driven by coverage, not bits
rows.kill_condition: round-1 FLAT/NEGATIVE with measured coverage up and density flat -> diversity hypothesis loses its support

rows.claim: Daemon job failures do NOT evidence a mid-run interruption threat to the multi-day v0 pretrain; the interruption budget is bounded.
rows.equation_or_invariant: pessimistic_interruptions(v0) = (unknown_failures/era_days) * run_days = (4/11.0) * 3.352 = 1.22; cost_per_interruption <= checkpoint_interval + restart (resume bit-exact)
rows.metric: June ember-era failure classes; evidenced mid-run deaths; pessimistic interruptions per v0 run
rows.baseline: June era 30/285 failed -> 8 NON-EMBER + 2 MISUSE + 14 FAST-FAIL-BOUNDED (gap<=600s) + 2 ROOT-CAUSED + 4 UNKNOWN-EVIDENCE-EXPIRED; evidenced mid-run deaths = 0; pessimistic budget 0.364/day -> 1.22 per 3.352-day run
rows.intervention: none on the daemon (fail-closed refusals are the contract working); per-job log_name already retains evidence forward; checkpoint cadence stays #231 measure-first with this row as its interruption-rate input
rows.kill_condition: row re-opens if any v0 segment dies mid-run without a receipted root cause

proof.rule: at least one row moved beyond diagnosis with before/after receipts under the same governor
proof.gain: tok_s_paced 17899.0 -> 24078.9 (1.345x vs reproduced anchor; 1.285x vs the original fp19 receipt value); pacing tax 0.2185 -> 0.049; projected v0 wall 4.509 -> 3.352 days (-1.157 days)
proof.deviation_status: config UNCHANGED; deviation PR gated behind E1b loss-match on real shards (queued behind #218 re-freeze)
